content:
- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and applied selection rules from user + AGENTS.
- Verified bug-first condition: all bug tasks are marked passing true; no failing bug tasks present.
- Found multiple non-bug tasks with <passes>false</passes>; selected the most logical next unblocked task in the verification flow: story-full-verification/02-task-run-targeted-unit-and-integration-tests.md.
- Wrote selected task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stopped.
- Next cycle should execute that task and re-evaluate task graph after status updates.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and loaded task `.ralph/tasks/story-full-verification/02-task-run-targeted-unit-and-integration-tests.md`.
- Confirmed task was pre-execution (`<status>not_started</status>`) with no lifecycle marker, so I ran planning-only phase.
- Gathered context in parallel: Makefile targets, add-bug skill schema, sibling task-01 completed format, marker-string expectations, and story directory naming.
- Wrote a full Draft 1 implementation plan into task-02, including evidence logging layout, serial gate order, failure triage/bug creation loop, rerun strategy, and finalization checklist.
- Added terminal marker `TO BE VERIFIED` at end of task file and intentionally did not execute tests/edits beyond planning, per user workflow.
- Next step for next turn: perform deep skeptical review of this draft plan, alter at least one plan element, and then replace end marker with `NOW EXECUTE` only when satisfied.
